title: FuelCell Energy Reports Second Quarter of Fiscal 2024 Results
text: FuelCell Energy Q2 2024 (ended Apr 30, 2024) revenue was $22.4 million, down 42% YoY from $38.3 million. Gross loss was $(7.1) million vs $(6.1) million prior year. Operating loss was $(41.4) million vs $(35.9) million prior year. Net loss per share was $(0.07), improved from $(0.09) prior year. Revenue breakdown: Service agreements $1.4 million (down from $26.2M, no module exchanges), Generation $14.1 million (up 67% from $8.4M), Advanced Technologies $6.9 million (up from $3.7M including ExxonMobil JDA and Esso purchase order). Cash position: Total $313.2 million; Unrestricted cash $158.8 million; Short-term investments $101.3 million; Restricted cash $53.1 million. Total backlog $1.06 billion (up 3.8% YoY from $1.02B). CEO Jason Few stated company continued executing on all three strategy pillars while maintaining cost discipline. Post-quarter, FuelCell announced $160 million agreement with South Korean firm Gyeonggi Green Energy for 42 fuel cell modules plus long-term maintenance services.
